title: smallanthus jelskii
intro: Smallanthus jelskii, also known as Jell's or Jerusalem artichoke, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant's roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber with medicinal properties, which can help regulate blood sugar levels and improve digestion. Consuming Smallanthus jelskii has been associated with various health benefits, including weight management, reduced inflammation, and improved gut health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or added to food as a flavoring agent, offering a natural way to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
